original_image_tags: Scanned document page, rotated 90 degrees clockwise. The page appears to be the back of a form or routing sheet, showing faint "bleed-through" text from the other side. The page is covered with numerous date/time stamps in blue and purple ink, indicating various receipt and routing dates in April and March of 1956. Handwritten text is visible in the bottom right corner.

elements.description: Page with multiple blue and black ink reception stamps from 1956. Visible text includes 'RECEIVED - BOARDMAIL' (multiple dates), 'RECEIVED - MOHR', 'REC'D ESPIONAGE F.B.I', 'REC'D BELMONT FBI - JUSTICE', and 'RECEIVED CH. 13. OFF'. Also features two punch holes at the top edge.

judge.reason: Description A states there are only blue and purple ink stamps and handwritten text, while Description B quotes several specific stamps (e.g., 'RECEIVED - BOARDMAIL', 'REC'D ESPIONAGE F.B.I') that A does not mention at all, indicating A is hallucinating the absence of clear text content.
